configuration_examples.overview: Complete examples demonstrating how to create REST connector configuration files for different types of APIs and scenarios.

example.name: simple_public_api
example.title: Simple Public API (No Authentication)
example.description: SpaceX API - a public REST API with no authentication required. Demonstrates basic endpoint mapping with various data types.
example.configuration: { "$connector_name": "SpaceX API Connector", "$connector_label": "SpaceX API", "$connector_description": "Connector for SpaceX public REST API - provides access to rockets, launches, capsules, and more", "$hostname": "https://api.spacexdata.com", "$authentication": "none", "$tables": { "ROCKETS": { "$path": ["/v4/rockets"], "id": "VARCHAR,$key", "name": "VARCHAR", "type": "VARCHAR", "active": "BOOLEAN", "stages": "INTEGER", "boosters": "INTEGER", "cost_per_launch": "BIGINT", "success_rate_pct": "INTEGER", "first_flight": "DATE", "country": "VARCHAR", "company": "VARCHAR", "height": "VARCHAR", "diameter": "VARCHAR", "mass": "VARCHAR", "engines": "VARCHAR", "flickr_images": "VARCHAR" } } }

decision.reason: String UUID used as primary key
Nested objects - stored as JSON strings

example.name: api_with_authentication
example.title: API with API Key Authentication
example.description: Example API requiring API Key authentication. Demonstrates authentication configuration.

decision.reason: API requires API Key in Authorization header
decision.note: User will provide api_key when creating connection

decision.reason: Numeric ID used as primary key
Nested object - stored as JSON string

example.name: api_without_natural_key
example.title: API Response Without Natural Unique Identifier
example.description: Polish National Bank API - returns data without natural unique IDs. Demonstrates using synthetic ROWID as primary key.

decision.reason: No natural unique identifier in response - synthetic key added
decision.note: Connector automatically generates unique ROWID for each row

decision.reason: Monetary value - use DECIMAL for precision

example.name: oauth2_authentication
example.title: API with OAuth2 Bearer Token
example.description: Example API using OAuth2 Bearer Token authentication.
example.note: User must obtain OAuth2 bearer token through their OAuth2 flow before creating connection. Token refresh is not currently supported by the connector.

example.name: basic_authentication
example.title: API with Basic Authentication
example.description: Example API using HTTP Basic authentication (username/password).
example.note: Connector automatically Base64-encodes username:password combination. User provides username and password separately when creating connection.

example.name: complex_nested_structures
example.title: API with Complex Nested Structures
example.description: Demonstrates handling of deeply nested objects and arrays. All nested structures are mapped as VARCHAR.

decision.reason: Deeply nested object with multiple levels - stored as JSON string
decision.wrong: Do NOT try to flatten into specifications_dimensions_width, etc.

decision.reason: Array of objects - stored as JSON string
decision.wrong: Do NOT create separate reviews table

decision.reason: Array of objects - stored as JSON string

example.important: ALL nested objects and arrays MUST be mapped as VARCHAR. The connector will store them as JSON strings that can be queried using JSON functions in SQL.

workflow_example.title: Complete Workflow: Creating Configuration for New API

step.title: User Request
step.user_message: I want to create a connector for the GitHub API to access repository information.

assistant_questions.question: What is the base URL of the GitHub API?
What authentication method does the GitHub API use?
Which specific endpoints do you want to map?
Can you provide an example JSON response from that endpoint?

step.title: Analyze Response
step.analysis: - Endpoint returns single repository object - Has natural unique identifier: "id" (integer) - Contains nested objects: owner, license, permissions - Contains arrays: topics - Mix of data types: strings, integers, booleans, timestamps

step.title: Explain Mapping
step.explanation: - Mapped endpoint to REPOSITORIES table - Used "id" as primary key (INTEGER,$key) - Mapped nested objects (owner, license) as VARCHAR - Mapped arrays (topics) as VARCHAR - Used appropriate types for simple fields

step.title: Confirm and Iterate
step.question: Does this configuration correctly represent the API structure?
step.if_changes_needed: Apply exact changes requested by user
